problem.title: Triangle
problem.description: Given a triangle array, return the minimum path sum from top to bottom.
assumptions: Move to adjacent numbers on the row below.

example.explanation: Path: 2→3→5→1 = 11. Bottom-up DP: for each row, update in-place adding minimum of two children below.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Starting from the bottom row, repeatedly update a 1-D DP array so that dp[j] holds the minimum path sum from position j in the current row to any leaf below. After processing all rows upward, dp[0] contains the global answer. The key insight is that bottom-up DP eliminates the need for memoization of intermediate results and allows in-place modification of a single array equal in size to the last row.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def minimum_total(triangle: List[List[int]]) -> int:
    # Copy the last row as the starting DP state
    dp = list(triangle[-1])

    # Process rows from second-to-last up to the apex
    for i in range(len(triangle) - 2, -1, -1):
        for j in range(i + 1):  # row i has i+1 elements
            dp[j] = min(dp[j], dp[j + 1]) + triangle[i][j]

    return dp[0]

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Bottom-Up In-Place DP [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Start from the second-to-last row of the triangle and work upward. For each cell triangle[i][j], add the minimum of its two children: triangle[i][j] += min(triangle[i+1][j], triangle[i+1][j+1]). After processing all rows, triangle[0][0] holds the minimum path sum. O(n^2) time O(1) extra space (modifies the input in place).
